currentTask.name: Add counsel model selection policy
currentTask.goal: Decide whether OpenRouter free-model counsel groupings are the right direction, then add an evidence-based selection policy without prematurely hardcoding live provider routing

environment.note: This workspace runs entirely on Debian/WSL2. No PowerShell needed.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Added slice-first workflow guardrails and committed them as e1299cf

Added milestone-shape.sh for turning big goals into bounded bets
Added optimize-gate.sh for evidence-based optimization routing
Extended task-intake.sh with north-star goal horizon detection
Updated start-task and shape-task behavior to make shaping the default first move for serious tasks
Updated workflow docs with big-goal execution diagrams and optimization lane guidance
Added docs/fast-stable-delivery.md to align the workflow with Working Backwards, Shape Up, DORA, and Trunk-Based Development
Updated workflow and overview docs to explicitly state that speed and stability should improve together through smaller verified batches
Added product-shape.sh to grill and compress the intended product experience before North Star and milestone shaping
Added counsel-gate.sh to decide when multi-perspective review is worth using
Added /shape-product and /counsel commands plus propagation entries
Changed task-intake so long-horizon goals route to /shape-product first
Added docs/counsel-model-selection.md with benchmark-informed evidence hierarchy
Added counsel-models.json as a refreshable snapshot of current role candidates
Added counsel-model-select.sh to explain lite/full counsel role selection policy
Confirmed model grouping is the right direction only as role-based, refreshable selection rather than fixed permanent panels
Updated history index and full detailed history to record the workflow evolution
